response.justification: The candidate is primarily a full-stack web developer whose core expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies. C++ experience is confined to a 6-month role at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in the skills section. There is no evidence of STL usage, multi-threaded systems, message broker architecture, MQTT, IEC 62443, secure development lifecycle practices, or any industrial automation / IoT background — all of which are central to this role.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven architecture experience through the GISCollective open-source platform
Strong software craftsmanship foundation (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, agile) across multiple employers
Exposure to low-latency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second
concerns: C++ is absent from the skills list; depth is limited to one 6-month position and a thesis — far below the 8–12 years of C++ expertise required
No knowledge of industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), SDL practices, or secure embedded/OT development
No experience with MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0), or IoT/industrial automation contexts
